rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL VS. THE SHEMANO GROUP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 12, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION are denied. Plaintiff presents triable issues of fact as to: the knowledge and conduct of Cacchione as to the control agreement and transfer of accounts to M.C.F.; whether such knowledge and conduct can be imputed to M.C.F.; whether the accounts were transferred in the ordinary course of business or through collusion between Cacchione and DeBraggio. While there may be some merit to the claim that the transfer was ratified, this also presents triable issues of fact regarding plaintiff's knowledge of the conversion and expressed consent to it. Defendants objections 1, 2, 3 are overruled. Objection 4 is sustained. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MCVEIGH VS. SF RECYCLING & DISPOSAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 12, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANTS SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY, RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO, RECOLOGY, INC., RECOLOGY SERVICE CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Summary judgment as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th causes of action granted. Plaintiff cannot demonstrate that he engaged in protected activity to support the false claims cause of action or Labor Code cause of action. Because Labor Code 1102.5 cause of action fails, then wrongful discharge in violation of Public Policy also fails. Motion for summary adjudication on implied contract cause of action granted based upon plaintiff's admission that he signed an at-will acknowledgment when he started working for defendant and failure to present sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of fact of a just cause/implied contract. =(302/LMG)
